info.name: DockOck MCP Server
info.description:
JSON-RPC 2.0 API for the DockOck MCP (Model Context Protocol) server.

Exposes document search and Q&A tools that external AI agents can consume.

**Prerequisites:**
1. Index documents in DockOck (run "Index Documents" with RAG enabled)
2. Start the MCP server via the 🔌 MCP button in the bottom bar

Default base URL: `http://localhost:3100`

request.description: Returns the service status and protocol version.
MCP handshake — returns server capabilities and version info. Call this first before using any tools.
List all available MCP tools with their names, descriptions, and input schemas.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Semantic search + LLM-generated answer.

Searches the indexed documents for relevant chunks, then sends them to the LLM to generate a natural language answer with source citations.

**Parameters:**
- `query` (string, required) — The question to ask
- `top_k` (integer, optional, default: 10) — Max source chunks to retrieve

**Response:**
- `content[].text` — The generated answer with sources
- `_meta.sources[]` — Structured source references (document, chunk_id, relevance, excerpt)

response.name: 200 OK — Answer with Sources

request.description:
Raw semantic search — returns matching document chunks with relevance scores. No LLM generation, so it's faster than query_documents.

**Parameters:**
- `query` (string, required) — Search query text
- `top_k` (integer, optional, default: 10) — Max results to return

**Response:**
- `content[].text` — Formatted search results
- `_meta.results[]` — Structured data (document, chunk_id, relevance, excerpt)

response.name: 200 OK — Search Results

request.description:
List all documents that have been indexed in the knowledge base, with their chunk counts.

No parameters required.

**Response:**
- `content[].text` — Formatted document list
- `_meta.documents[]` — Structured data (file_name, chunk_count)

response.name: 200 OK — Document List

request.description: Demonstrates the error response when calling an unknown method.
Demonstrates the error when calling query_documents without a query parameter.
